# John Kirima — Personal Landing Page

*A minimal, brutalist personal landing page built from scratch to serve as a digital home for my work in Business Analytics and Data Science.*

## Description

*What the project does, why it exists, and who it is for.*

This is a single-page static website designed to showcase my skills, projects, and professional experience to recruiters, hiring managers, and peers. It replaces the traditional resume format with a living, accessible web presence. Built entirely with vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript — no frameworks, no templates, no shortcuts.

## Features

*Key capabilities from the user's perspective.*

- **Brutalist / terminal-inspired design** — dark theme, monospace typography, ASCII decorative elements
- **Fully responsive** — optimized for mobile (320px+), tablet, and desktop viewports
- **WCAG 2.2 Level AA accessible** — semantic HTML, proper heading hierarchy, aria-labels, keyboard navigable
- **Smooth scroll navigation** — fixed bottom nav bar with section anchors
- **Project showcase** — numbered project cards with tech tags and external links
- **Professional headshot integration** — rectangular, no-frills image treatment
- **Lightweight and fast** — no build tools, no dependencies, no bloat
- **SEO-ready** — meta description, semantic markup, proper document structure

## Tech Stack

*Every language, library, framework, and tool used.*

| Technology | Purpose |
|---|---|
| HTML5 | Semantic page structure and content |
| CSS3 | Styling, layout (Grid, Flexbox), responsive design, animations |
| JavaScript (Vanilla) | Smooth scroll navigation fallback |
| Google Fonts (Space Mono) | Monospace typeface for brutalist aesthetic |
| Git / GitHub | Version control and source code hosting |
| Azure Static Web Apps | Production hosting and deployment |

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

*What you need installed before running the project.*

- A modern web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
- [Git](https://git-scm.com/downloads) (optional, for cloning)
- A code editor like [VS Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/) (optional, for editing)

### Installation

*Step-by-step instructions to clone the repo and run it locally.*

1. Clone the repository:
   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/johnkirima/jkirima-personalanding-page.git
   ```
2. Navigate to the project folder:
   ```bash
   cd jkirima-personalanding-page
   ```
3. Open `index.html` in your browser:
   ```bash
   open index.html
   ```
   Or simply double-click the `index.html` file in your file explorer.

## Usage

*How to use the application once it is running.*

The site is a single-page layout. Use the fixed navigation bar at the bottom of the screen to jump between sections: Home, About, Skills, Projects, Experience, Education, and Contact. All external links (LinkedIn, GitHub, X, email) open in new tabs. The site is fully keyboard-navigable — use Tab to move between interactive elements.

## Project Structure

*Main folders and files with their purposes.*

```
jkirima-personalanding-page/
├── index.html              # Main HTML page — all content lives here
├── css/
│   └── stylesheet.css      # All styles — brutalist theme, responsive layout
├── js/
│   └── scripts.js          # Smooth scroll navigation (vanilla JS)
├── images/
│   └── headshot.jpg        # Professional headshot used in About section
├── PRD.md                  # Product Requirements Document
├── STANDARDS.md            # Technical and design standards
├── WORKING_NOTES.md        # Internal working notes and session log
├── README.md               # This file
└── .gitignore              # Git ignore rules
```

## Changelog

*Release history.*

### v1.1.0 — 2026-03-20

- Added proper professional headshot to About section (restructured to photo + content two-column layout)
- Added dedicated Education section (University of Iowa, Kirkwood Community College, Google certifications)
- Added Multi-Ownership in European Soccer as Project 04 (Tableau / Kaggle)
- Updated Projects section to five cards total with fuller descriptions from resume
- Updated Skills categories to match resume exactly (Programming & Databases / Data & Visualization / AI & Machine Learning)
- Updated navigation bar to include Education anchor link
- Fixed workflow startup command to auto-accept package installation
- Imported and configured on Replit with static deployment settings

### v1.0.0 — 2026-03-19

- Initial release
- Hero section with animated blinking cursor
- About section with headshot and stats
- Skills section with three-column grid (Languages, Tools, Domains)
- Projects section with four project cards (DataForge, Incident Dashboard, Spotify Analysis, Landing Page)
- Experience section with timeline layout
- Contact section with social links
- Fixed bottom navigation bar
- Fully responsive from 320px to desktop
- Deployed to Azure Static Web Apps

## Known Issues / To-Do

- [ ] Add favicon and Open Graph meta tags for better social sharing
- [ ] Add dark/light theme toggle
- [ ] Add a blog or writing section for technical articles
- [ ] Improve print stylesheet for resume-like output
- [ ] Add page load animations and scroll-triggered reveals

## Roadmap

*Future improvements for hypothetical next versions.*

- **v1.1** — Add Open Graph and Twitter Card meta tags for social media previews
- **v1.2** — Implement a project detail modal or expandable cards
- **v1.3** — Add a blog/writing section with Markdown-to-HTML rendering
- **v2.0** — Build a CMS-backed version with dynamic content updates
- **v2.1** — Add analytics tracking (privacy-respecting, e.g., Plausible)

## Contributing

*How to fork, change, and submit a pull request.*

Contributions are welcome. If you'd like to suggest improvements or report issues:

1. Fork the repository
2. Create a feature branch (`git checkout -b feature/your-feature`)
3. Commit your changes (`git commit -m 'Add your feature'`)
4. Push to the branch (`git push origin feature/your-feature`)
5. Open a Pull Request
